title: Inventory Tools Reference Capture Surface
summary: Current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the tools/reference/ capture surface that prior plan_fps work used to prove reference oracle capture is feasible: tools/reference/isolationProbe.ts (296 lines, sandbox-creation + per-file SHA-256 verification + config-file true-copy isolation + cleanup), and tools/reference/windowCaptureProbe.ts (488 lines, GDI window-capture pipeline configuration + 12-step BitBlt/GetDIBits ordering + Win32 BITMAPINFOHEADER builder + Bun:FFI USER32/GDI32 dlopen feasibility proof + live captureDesktopRegion). Both probe modules are read-only utilities exposed via named exports for consumption by the corresponding companion tests at test/oracles/reference-run-isolation.test.ts (259 lines, exercises probeIsolation and createSandbox) and test/oracles/window-capture-feasibility.test.ts (434 lines, exercises buildBitmapInfoHeader, validateCaptureConfig, captureDesktopRegion, plus Win32 constant invariants). The inventory also captures the tools-root-level non-capture modules tools/verify.ts (91 lines, the canonical bun-test-then-typecheck three-command verification sequence used by every Ralph-loop step) and tools/format-changed.ts (64 lines, the bun-only Biome formatter dispatcher invoked by the package.json `format` script) plus the companion test test/scaffold/verify-commands.test.ts (229 lines, exercises buildVerifySequence and the VERIFY_COMMAND_KINDS/VERIFY_COMMAND_COUNT/TSCONFIG_PATH constants). The capture pipeline pins the CHOCOLATE_DOOM_WINDOW_TITLE='Chocolate Doom 2.2.1' string, the 640x480x32bpp display dimensions, the 320x200 internal Doom framebuffer downscale target, the 32-bits-per-pixel BGRA capture buffer of 640*480*4=1228800 bytes, the 12-step ordered GDI capture sequence (FindWindowW → GetClientRect → GetDC → CreateCompatibleDC → CreateCompatibleBitmap → SelectObject → BitBlt → GetDIBits → SelectObject → DeleteObject → DeleteDC → ReleaseDC) using @bun-win32/user32 and @bun-win32/gdi32 packages, the 11-unique-API surface (BitBlt, CreateCompatibleBitmap, CreateCompatibleDC, DeleteDC, DeleteObject, FindWindowW, GetClientRect, GetDC, GetDIBits, ReleaseDC, SelectObject), the SRCCOPY=0x00cc0020 / DIB_RGB_COLORS=0 / BI_RGB=0 raster-operation and DIB-usage constants, the BITMAPINFOHEADER_SIZE=40 packed-struct byte total, the CAPTURE_BITS_PER_PIXEL=32 / CAPTURE_BYTES_PER_PIXEL=4 derived widths, and the USER32_CAPTURE_SYMBOLS / GDI32_CAPTURE_SYMBOLS bun:ffi dlopen symbol tables (5 user32 entries: FindWindowW, GetClientRect, GetDC, GetDesktopWindow, ReleaseDC; 7 gdi32 entries: BitBlt, CreateCompatibleBitmap, CreateCompatibleDC, DeleteDC, DeleteObject, GetDIBits, SelectObject) — every Win32 handle declared as FFIType.u64 per the AGENTS.md FFI rule. The sandbox isolation probe pins the REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4 / MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 / IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 sandbox-content invariants (mutable: chocolate-doom.cfg + default.cfg; immutable: DOOM.EXE + DOOM1.WAD), the SHA-256 verification per file, the size verification per file, the config-file true-copy isolation check (write a sentinel to the sandbox copy, assert the source bundle does NOT contain the sentinel, restore the sandbox copy), the source-unmodified post-condition check (re-hash every source file after the probe and confirm the pre/post hashes match), and the recursive sandbox-directory cleanup via fs/promises.rm. The verification command sequence pins the canonical three-command tuple (focused-test, full-suite, typecheck) with VERIFY_COMMAND_COUNT=3 and TSCONFIG_PATH at the package root, exposed via buildVerifySequence(focusedTestPath) returning a frozen array of {kind, description, command='bun', args, order} VerifyCommand entries that every Ralph-loop step executes in fixed order. The inventory does not embed proprietary id Software bytes; the probe modules and tests reference the doom/ reference bundle via the read-only REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY/SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES bundle policy at src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts and the REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST at src/oracles/referenceRunManifest.ts.
evidence_method: inventory lane local repository audit using Bun.Glob-equivalent readdirSync enumeration of tools/, tools/reference/, statSync for size_bytes, line counting via readFileSync split('\n') with trailing-newline normalization, sha256sum-equivalent SHA-256 fingerprints over the raw file bytes, regex extraction of `^export (const|function|interface|type|class)` declarations from each TypeScript module (per the prior 01-013 inventory pattern), Grep enumeration of importing files to derive src_importer_count and test_importer_count, and JSON-encoded constant capture for the GDI capture pipeline (display/internal dimensions, bits per pixel, capture buffer size, ordered step list, required API set, package mapping) and the sandbox required-file counts (mutable + immutable). Companion test files at test/oracles/reference-run-isolation.test.ts and test/oracles/window-capture-feasibility.test.ts and test/scaffold/verify-commands.test.ts are inventoried for size_bytes, line_count, and sha256 only; their internal test-case enumeration is owned by the prior 01-016 fixture-locking parity-test inventory and the prior 01-002 simplified-launcher inventory respectively. plan_engine/ and plan_fps/ are listed as read-only paths in the step contract but are NOT enumerated here — they are prior art under plan_vanilla_parity governance and any tools/ symbol they reference is already pinned by the prior 01-001 root-script inventory, the prior 01-013 save-and-config inventory, or the prior 01-014 demo-and-replay inventory, none of which this step modifies.

capture_modules.role: Implements the sandbox-isolation feasibility probe for reference oracle capture: probeIsolation() creates a temporary sandbox under REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY.workspaceRoot/sandboxParent via fs/promises.mkdtemp, copies every SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES entry from the read-only doom/ reference bundle into the sandbox via Bun.write, computes per-file SHA-256 (uppercase hex via Bun.CryptoHasher) and size_bytes for FileHashResult records, performs config-file true-copy isolation by appending a `# isolation-probe-sentinel` line to the sandbox copy and asserting the source bundle does NOT contain the sentinel before restoring, hashes the source bundle files before and after the probe to confirm sourceUnmodified, then deletes the sandbox tree via fs/promises.rm({recursive:true,force:true}) when REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY.cleanupAfterRun is true. The probe exposes the REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4 / MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 / IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 sandbox-content invariants derived from the SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES policy and the auxiliary createSandbox() helper for tests that need a populated sandbox without the full integrity probe.

Implements the GDI window-capture feasibility probe for reference oracle framebuffer capture: declares the SRCCOPY=0x00cc0020 raster-op constant, the DIB_RGB_COLORS=0 / BI_RGB=0 GetDIBits usage flags, the BITMAPINFOHEADER_SIZE=40 packed-struct byte total and BITMAPINFOHEADER_OFFSETS frozen field-offset table, the CHOCOLATE_DOOM_WINDOW_TITLE='Chocolate Doom 2.2.1' window-title literal, the CAPTURE_BITS_PER_PIXEL=32 / CAPTURE_BYTES_PER_PIXEL=4 derived widths, the CAPTURE_PIPELINE frozen configuration (640x480 display, 320x200 internal, 1228800-byte capture buffer, window title, 12-step ordered pipeline), the CAPTURE_STEPS array of 12 frozen CaptureStep records pinning the canonical Win32 capture order (FindWindowW → GetClientRect → GetDC → CreateCompatibleDC → CreateCompatibleBitmap → SelectObject → BitBlt → GetDIBits → SelectObject restore → DeleteObject → DeleteDC → ReleaseDC), the REQUIRED_API_FUNCTIONS frozen 11-element set of unique API names, the API_PACKAGE_MAP ReadonlyMap from API name to bun-win32 package (@bun-win32/user32 or @bun-win32/gdi32), the USER32_CAPTURE_SYMBOLS frozen 5-entry FFI symbol table (FindWindowW, GetClientRect, GetDC, GetDesktopWindow, ReleaseDC) and GDI32_CAPTURE_SYMBOLS frozen 7-entry FFI symbol table (BitBlt, CreateCompatibleBitmap, CreateCompatibleDC, DeleteDC, DeleteObject, GetDIBits, SelectObject) using FFIType.u64 for every Win32 handle, the buildBitmapInfoHeader(width,height) helper that allocates a 40-byte little-endian Buffer with biSize=40, biWidth=width, biHeight=-height (top-down), biPlanes=1, biBitCount=32, biCompression=BI_RGB, biSizeImage=width*height*4, the validateCaptureConfig(screen) helper that compares CAPTURE_PIPELINE against a ScreenParameters record from referenceRunManifest.ts and returns an array of inconsistency descriptions, and the captureDesktopRegion(width,height) live-capture function that runs the full GetDesktopWindow → GetDC → CreateCompatibleDC → CreateCompatibleBitmap → SelectObject → BitBlt → GetDIBits pipeline via direct bun:ffi dlopen calls with finally-block handle release for resource leak prevention.

tools_root_modules.role: Implements the bun-only Biome formatter dispatcher invoked by the package.json `format` script: enumerates `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRTUXB` (working tree), `git diff --cached` (index), and `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` (untracked) via collectGitPaths, filters out READ_ONLY_ROOTS (doom/, iwad/, reference/) regardless of source, restricts untracked enumeration to UNTRACKED_FORMAT_ROOTS (plan_fps/, plan_vanilla_parity/, src/, test/, tools/) plus UNTRACKED_FORMAT_FILES (biome.json, package.json, tsconfig.json), normalizes Windows-style backslash path separators to forward slashes, sorts the deduplicated path set ASCIIbetically, and spawns `bun x biome format --write --no-errors-on-unmatched <paths>` via Bun.spawn to reformat only the changed files in place.

Implements the canonical three-command verification sequence contract that every Ralph-loop step runs after editing: defines the VerifyCommandKind type discriminant ('focused-test' | 'full-suite' | 'typecheck'), the frozen VERIFY_COMMAND_KINDS array pinning the mandatory execution order, the VERIFY_COMMAND_COUNT=3 cardinality, the VerifyCommand interface (kind, description, command='bun', args, order), the buildVerifySequence(focusedTestPath) factory that emits a frozen 3-element array with focused-test args=['test', focusedTestPath], full-suite args=['test'], and typecheck args=['x', 'tsc', '--noEmit', '--project', TSCONFIG_PATH], the TSCONFIG_PATH constant resolved relative to the package root via node:path.resolve, and the verifyPackageRoot() helper that returns the absolute package-root path. The module has no runtime side effects; it is pure metadata that callers (and the .claude/skills/verify-step skill) consume to enforce the canonical bun-only verify ordering without depending on shell-specific quoting.

capture_pipeline_invariants.notes: The capture pipeline is the GDI feasibility proof that the Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 window's client area can be read into a 32-bit BGRA pixel buffer matching the reference run manifest screen parameters. The captureBufferSize=1228800 is the exact 640*480*4 product the test recomputes on every run. The 12-step capture order pins the canonical GDI screen-capture sequence with the SelectObject restore step before DeleteObject; any reorder would either leak a GDI handle (no DeleteObject before DeleteDC) or read garbled pixels (BitBlt before SelectObject). The 11-unique-API surface count derives from collapsing the SelectObject pair (steps 6 and 9) into a single API name. The user32 symbol table includes GetDesktopWindow because captureDesktopRegion uses the desktop window as a stand-in for live capture verification when the Chocolate Doom window is not running.

sandbox_invariants.notes: The sandbox-isolation probe enforces a four-axis contract: (a) every required file is present after copy (REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4); (b) every required file's SHA-256 in the sandbox matches the policy's expectedHash (allHashesMatch); (c) every mutable config file in the sandbox is a true copy that does not back-write to the source bundle (allConfigsIsolated); (d) every source bundle file's SHA-256 is unchanged after the probe runs (sourceUnmodified). The probe writes a temporary sandbox under REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY.workspaceRoot/sandboxParent (with a .gitkeep created and removed by the probe to ensure the parent exists for mkdtemp), uses a unique sandboxPrefix from the policy, and removes the sandbox tree via fs/promises.rm({recursive:true,force:true}) when cleanupAfterRun is true. The four required files are exactly the SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES policy entries (mutable: chocolate-doom.cfg + default.cfg; immutable: DOOM.EXE + DOOM1.WAD); this inventory does not embed the policy's expectedHash or expectedSize values because they are pinned by the prior 02-001 catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs step under the oracle lane, not the inventory lane.

verify_command_invariants.notes: The canonical verification command sequence is exposed via tools/verify.ts buildVerifySequence(focusedTestPath) returning a frozen 3-element array of {kind, command='bun', args, order} VerifyCommand records. The bun-only enforcement matches the AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md runtime constraints: there is no fallback to node, ts-node, npx, vitest, jest, or mocha. Every Ralph-loop step's verification commands resolve to bun + this exact arg vector — `bun test <focused-path>` then `bun test` then `bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json`. The tsconfig path is resolved at module load time relative to the tools/ directory's parent (the package root) via node:path.resolve(import.meta.dir, '..').

boundary_status.notes: tools/ and tools/reference/ are writable workspaces under plan_vanilla_parity governance. The tools/reference/ probe modules read the doom/ reference bundle via the read-only REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY at src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts (which itself enumerates the bundle's mutable + immutable file set), but they never modify the bundle: probeIsolation() copies bundle bytes into a writable sandbox under workspaceRoot/sandboxParent, hashes the copies, and removes the sandbox; captureDesktopRegion() never touches doom/ at all (it captures live desktop pixels from a Win32 process). This inventory captures only filename, relative path, size_bytes, line_count, sha256 fingerprint, and the GDI/sandbox/verify-command invariants — no proprietary id Software bytes are embedded. The companion test files at test/oracles/reference-run-isolation.test.ts and test/oracles/window-capture-feasibility.test.ts and test/scaffold/verify-commands.test.ts are inventoried with the same four filename/size/line/sha256 axes; their internal test-case enumeration is the responsibility of the prior 01-002 simplified-launcher inventory and the prior 01-016 fixture-locking parity-test inventory, neither of which this step modifies.

implications: The tools/reference/ capture surface is the feasibility proof that prior plan_fps work used to demonstrate that reference oracle capture is mechanically possible: the isolationProbe.ts module proves the doom/ reference bundle can be copied into a sandboxed working directory, integrity-verified via SHA-256, used in isolation, and torn down without modifying the source bytes; the windowCaptureProbe.ts module proves the Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 window's client area can be read into a 640x480x32bpp BGRA pixel buffer via the canonical 12-step GDI BitBlt/GetDIBits pipeline using @bun-win32/user32 + @bun-win32/gdi32 packages with FFIType.u64 handles. plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-capture steps (02-005 capture-doomd-clean-launch-feasibility through 02-035 gate-oracle-foundation-without-deferred-status) inherit this feasibility proof and must not regress the capture invariants pinned in this inventory: the 12-step GDI capture order, the 11-unique-API surface, the 4-file sandbox content (2 mutable + 2 immutable), the 1228800-byte capture buffer size, and the sourceUnmodified post-condition.

The tools/verify.ts module pins the canonical bun-test-then-typecheck three-command verification contract (focused-test, full-suite, typecheck in that fixed order) that every Ralph-loop step under plan_vanilla_parity executes after editing. The VERIFY_COMMAND_COUNT=3 and VERIFY_COMMAND_KINDS frozen array are non-negotiable: any future change to the verification contract (for example adding a fourth lint step or reordering typecheck before full-suite) would need to update both tools/verify.ts and the test/scaffold/verify-commands.test.ts companion test, and would propagate to every step file's `## verification commands` section. The bun-only enforcement matches the AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md runtime constraint that NEVER permits node, ts-node, npx, vitest, jest, or mocha.

The tools/format-changed.ts module is the dispatcher behind the package.json `format` script (the `bun run format` command that the canonical verify sequence runs first). It enforces the read-only-roots policy (doom/, iwad/, reference/) by skipping any path under those directories regardless of git source, restricting untracked-file formatting to plan_fps/, plan_vanilla_parity/, src/, test/, tools/ plus the three explicitly-named root files biome.json/package.json/tsconfig.json. Future Ralph-loop steps that introduce a new top-level writable directory (for example a hypothetical scripts/ or docs/) would need to update tools/format-changed.ts UNTRACKED_FORMAT_ROOTS or accept that untracked files in that new directory will not be auto-formatted on commit.

The CHOCOLATE_DOOM_WINDOW_TITLE='Chocolate Doom 2.2.1' string is the load-bearing window-title literal that windowCaptureProbe.ts FindWindowW uses to locate the live reference process. Future plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-capture steps that bind to a different reference build (for example doomd.exe under DOSBox or a Chocolate Doom 3.0.x release) would need a new probe module with a different windowTitle constant rather than a parameterized override of this one — the constant is intentionally frozen to the 2.2.1 release the doom/ reference bundle ships.

The GDI capture pipeline's 12-step ordered sequence and the FFIType.u64 handle invariant for every Win32 handle (HWND, HDC, HBITMAP, HGDIOBJ) match the AGENTS.md FFI rules and CLAUDE.md FFI rules for src/host/win32/ and src/launcher/win32.ts. Future plan_vanilla_parity Phase 03 launch-lane steps that wire a live framebuffer capture into the doom_codex implementation must reuse the same 11-API surface from the same two bun-win32 packages and must not introduce a different capture path (for example DXGI desktop duplication or GDI+ direct pixel access) without an explicit feasibility step that proves the alternative captures the same bytes the GetDIBits path produces.

The sandbox isolation probe's REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4 / MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 / IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 invariants and the per-file SHA-256 verification + size verification + config-file true-copy isolation + sourceUnmodified post-condition together form the integrity contract that any plan_vanilla_parity oracle-capture run must satisfy before its captured evidence can be trusted. Future Phase 02 capture steps that need to write a new mutable file into the sandbox (for example a savegame.dsg snapshot or a screenshot.bmp) would need to extend SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES at src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts (which is owned by the oracle lane, not this inventory lane) and propagate the new MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT and IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT through both probe code and this inventory snapshot.

Because this inventory records on-disk SHA-256 fingerprints for every probe module and every companion test, any structural rewrite of tools/reference/isolationProbe.ts, tools/reference/windowCaptureProbe.ts, tools/verify.ts, tools/format-changed.ts, or any of the three companion tests will diverge from the captured snapshot and require a follow-up update to this inventory. The focused inventory test enforces that divergence by recomputing each fingerprint at test time against the live filesystem, so a stale inventory will fail rather than silently mask a probe rewrite that breaks the capture contract. This includes Prettier or Biome reformatting passes that change whitespace — the inventory is intentionally byte-exact.
